decision_axes.prompt: Which essay advances a defensible literary claim about what the text means, beyond restating events?
Which essay sustains that claim across the whole response with specific, explained textual evidence?
Is the cleaner essay winning on surface form, length, formula, or topic sentences rather than meaning?
Is a rougher essay advancing a genuinely deeper interpretation that the broad pass may have discounted?
If the essays argue different themes, which theme is more textually grounded?
Do sentence-level errors make the analysis unrecoverable?
Is either response an unfinished scaffold that should be floored below complete essays?

anchors.title: Deeper interpretation with weaker proof
anchors.decision_rule: A rougher or more sophisticated interpretation beats a formulaic essay only when it is supported by at least two sustained moments from the text.

anchors.title: Mature theme vocabulary is not proof
anchors.decision_rule: Words like trauma, healing, identity, trust, accountability, or support do not count unless the student connects them to specific events and explains how the events prove the claim.

anchors.title: Full proof beats narrower sophistication
anchors.decision_rule: A complete, multi-event proof of an on-prompt consequence claim can beat a rougher essay whose deeper theme is mostly asserted.

anchors.title: Consequences, accountability, and trust are co-equal frames
anchors.decision_rule: A trauma/healing or trust/accountability reading wins over a consequences reading only when it is better developed, not because it sounds deeper.

anchors.title: Five-paragraph control is not decisive
anchors.decision_rule: Topic-sentence scaffolding is positive only when it also sustains interpretive work.

anchors.title: Plot summary ceiling
anchors.decision_rule: Organized summary of events without explanation of meaning caps a paper below essays that explain why events matter.

anchors.title: Mechanics threshold
anchors.decision_rule: Mechanics are decisive only when they make the analysis hard to follow; otherwise they are secondary.

anchors.title: Completion floor for scaffolds
anchors.decision_rule: Scaffold remnants place below all complete essays unless the complete essays are off-task.
